title: Proc vs Lambda 🥊
description: The subtle but crucial differences between the two sibling data types.

slides.title: If they are twins... Why two? 🧬
slides.content:
Procs and Lambdas act identically 90% of the time. But there are two extremely critical differences that determine when you use which.

These differences are standard interview questions for Junior Ruby roles!

slides.title: Difference 1: Arguments 🛡️
slides.content:
Lambdas are STRICT about arguments. Procs are LAZY.

Run this code! The Lambda crashes because we forgot to pass an argument. The Proc just silently replaces it with `nil` and keeps going.
slides.codeSnippet:
lazy_proc = Proc.new { |x| puts "Value is #{x.inspect}" }
strict_lambda = ->(x) { puts "Value is #{x.inspect}" }

puts "Calling Proc with no arguments:"
lazy_proc.call()

puts "\nCalling Lambda with no arguments:"
# This will crash instantly!
strict_lambda.call()

quizData.question: Which one will instantly crash (ArgumentError) if you pass it the wrong number of arguments?
quizData.explanation: Lambdas expect to be treated like actual methods, so they demand their arguments to be strictly perfect. Procs are just loose blocks of code.

slides.title: Difference 2: The Return Keyword 🛑
slides.content:
Lambdas and Procs handle the `return` keyword drastically differently. This is confusing, so listen closely:

If a **Lambda** hits `return`, it hands the value backward to the method that called the lambda, allowing the rest of the method to continue.

If a **Proc** hits `return`, it instantly permanently terminates NOT ONLY its own code, but heavily destroys the ENTIRE method it was called inside of as well!

slides.title: Return Logic in Action
slides.content: Run this! When the method hits the Proc, the Proc rips the method apart completely, so "I survived" never prints.
slides.codeSnippet:
def proc_tester
  my_proc = Proc.new { return "Proc Return!" }
  my_proc.call
  
  puts "I survived hitting the proc!"
end

def lambda_tester
  my_lambda = ->() { return "Lambda Return!" }
  my_lambda.call
  
  puts "I survived hitting the lambda!"
end

proc_tester
puts "---"
lambda_tester

quizData.question: What happens when a Proc block hits the explicit `return` keyword?

options.text: It passes data back to the enclosing method like normal.
It completely aborts/destroys both itself AND the method it's trapped inside of, taking everything down with it.

quizData.explanation: Because a Proc thinks it's just raw code pasted directly into the method, hitting `return` inside a proc forces the entire parent method to return and die.

slides.title: The Rule of Thumb 👍
slides.content: Because they act exactly like normal methods, you should realistically always use Lambdas. Only use Procs if you have a very highly specific use case for their weird behavior.
